volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: Curculio (Section 1.3.200 - Section 2.3.290)

sections.label: Section 1.3.200
sections.original: hocine fieri , ut inmodestis hic te moderes moribus ?
sections.translation: Is it right that you should behave yourself in this unreasonable manner ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.201
sections.original: Auro contra cedo modestum amatorem : a me aurum accipe .
sections.translation: Find me a reasonable lover against his weight in gold ; here , take the gold of me .

sections.label: Section 1.3.202
sections.original: Cedo mihi contra aurichalco cui ego sano serviam .
sections.translation: Do you find me a person for me to serve in his sound senses against his weight in double-distilled gold .

sections.label: Section 1.3.203
sections.original: Bene vale , ocule mi , nam sonitum et crepitum claustrorum audio ,
sections.translation: Kindly fare you well , apple of my eye , for I hear the sound and creaking of doors ; I think the keeper [*] is opening the temple . But , prithee , in this same manner

sections.label: Section 1.3.205
sections.original: inter nos amore utemur semper surrepticio ?
sections.translation: shall we always enjoy our love by stealth ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.206
sections.original: Minime , nam parasitum misi nudiusquartus Cariam
sections.translation: Far from it ; for I sent my Parasite four days since to Caria to fetch some money ; he ’ ll be here to-day .

sections.label: Section 1.3.207b
sections.original: Nimium consultas diu .
sections.translation: You are very long in your contriving .

sections.label: Section 1.3.208
sections.original: Ita me Venus amet , ut ego te hoc triduom numquam sinam
sections.translation: So may Venus love me , I ’ ll never allow you to be three days in this house here , before I procure your liberty .

sections.label: Section 1.3.210
sections.original: Facito ut memineris . tene etiam , prius quam hinc abeo , savium .
sections.translation: Take care to remember it . Once more , before I go hence , take this kiss .

sections.label: Section 1.3.211
sections.original: Siquidem herclé mihi regnum detur , numquam id potius persequar .
sections.translation: By heavens , really if a kingdom now were offered me , I should not obtain it with greater pleasure . When shall I see you again ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.212b
sections.original: Em istoc verbo vindictam para :
sections.translation: Why now , for that expression get ready the Praetor ’ s rod [*] ; if you love me , purchase my freedom ; don ’ t make any haggling . Take care to prevail with your offer . Kindly adieu ! ( Goes into the house of the PROCURER .)

sections.label: Section 1.3.214b
sections.original: Iamne ego relinquor ? pulcre , Palinure , occidi .
sections.translation: And am I then left behind ? Palinurus , I ’ m Lilled outright .

sections.label: Section 1.3.215
sections.original: Ego quidem , qui et vapulando et somno pereo .
sections.translation: And I as well , who am dying with thumps and sleepiness .

sections.label: Section 1.3.215b
sections.original: Sequere me .—
sections.translation: Do you follow me .

sections.label: Section 2.1.216
sections.original: Migrare certumst iam nunc e fano foras ,
sections.translation: I am resolved to depart from this Temple out of doors forthwith , since thus I find [*] the determination of Aesculapius , who sets me at nought , and chooses not that I should be healed . My health is declining , my weakness increases .

sections.label: Section 2.1.220
sections.original: nam iam quasi zona liene cinctus ambulo ,
sections.translation: For now I walk , girded with my spleen as though with a belt ; in my stomach do I seem to be holding a twin offspring . I ’ m afraid of nothing , but that , in my misery , I should burst asunder in the middle .

sections.label: Section 2.1.223
sections.original: Si recte facias , Phaedrome , auscultes mihi
sections.translation: If you do right , Phaedromus , you ’ ll listen to me , and banish this sorrow from your feelings .

sections.label: Section 2.1.225
sections.original: paves , parasitus quia non rediit Caria :
sections.translation: You are anxious because your Parasite hasn ’ t returned from Caria . I think he ’ ll bring the money ; but if he doesn ’ t bring it , by a chain of iron he couldn ’ t be withheld from betaking himself to eat at his manger [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.1.229
sections.original: Quis hic est qui loquitur ?
sections.translation: What person is it that speaks ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.229b
sections.original: Quoiam vocem ego audio ?
sections.translation: Whose voice is it that I hear ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.230
sections.original: Estne hic Palinurus Phaedromi ?
sections.translation: Isn ’ t this Palinurus , the servant of Phaedromus ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.230b
sections.original: Quis hic est homo
sections.translation: Who is this fellow with extended paunch , and eyes as green as grass ? From his figure I know him ; from his complexion I cannot recognize him . O , now I do know him : it ’ s the Procurer Cappadox . I ’ ll accost him .

sections.label: Section 2.1.234b
sections.original: Salve , Palinure .
sections.translation: Save you , Palinurus .

sections.label: Section 2.1.234c
sections.original: O scelerum caput ,
sections.translation: O source of villanies , save you ; how are you ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.235b
sections.original: Vivo .
sections.translation: I ’ m just alive .

sections.label: Section 2.1.235c
sections.original: Nempe ut dignus es .
sections.translation: Just as you deserve , I suppose ? But what ’ s the matter with you ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.236b
sections.original: Lien enecat , renes dolent ,
sections.translation: My spleen is killing me , my reins are in torment , my lungs are being torn asunder , my liver is being tortured , my heart-strings are giving way , all my intestines are in pain .

sections.label: Section 2.1.239
sections.original: Tum te igitur morbus agitat hepatiarius .
sections.translation: The liver complaint is afflicting you , then .

sections.label: Section 2.1.240
sections.original: Facile est miserum inridere .
sections.translation: ’ Tis an easy matter to laugh at the afflicted .

sections.label: Section 2.1.240b
sections.original: Quin tu aliquot dies
sections.translation: Well , then , do you hold out [*] for some days until your intestines become putrid . Now , while the humours are pretty sound , if you do that , you yourself might sell for a worse price than those intestines of yours .

sections.label: Section 2.1.244
sections.original: Lien díerectust .
sections.translation: My spleen is expanded .

sections.label: Section 2.1.244b
sections.original: Ambula , id lieni optumumst .
sections.translation: Take walking exercise [*] ; that ’ s the best thing for the spleen .

sections.label: Section 2.1.245
sections.original: Aufér istaec , quaeso , atque hoc responde quod rogo .
sections.translation: Prithee , have done with this , and answer me this which I ask ; can you possibly form a conjecture on it , if I relate to you what I dreamt last night in my sleep ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.248
sections.original: Vah , solus hic homost qui sciat divinitus .
sections.translation: Psha , this — ( pointing to himself ) — this is the sole person that is really skilled in divination ; why , the interpreters of dreams ask advice of myself ;

sections.label: Section 2.1.250
sections.original: quod eis respondi , ea omnes stant sententia .
sections.translation: the answer that I have given them , by that opinion they all stand .

sections.label: Section 2.2.251
sections.original: Palinure , quid stas ? quin depromuntur mihi
sections.translation: Palinurus , why do you delay ? Why are not the things served out for me which are needed for the breakfast to be prepared for the Parasite when he comes .

sections.label: Section 2.2.253b
sections.original: Mane sis , dum huic conicio somnium .
sections.translation: Wait , please , until I interpret his dream . ( Pointing to CAPPADOX .)

sections.label: Section 2.2.254
sections.original: Tute ipse , si quid somniasti , ad me refers .
sections.translation: Why , you your own self , if you ’ ve had any dream , always apply to me .

sections.label: Section 2.2.255
sections.original: Fateór .
sections.translation: I confess it .

sections.label: Section 2.2.255b
sections.original: Abi , déprome .
sections.translation: Be off , then , and serve out the things .

sections.label: Section 2.2.255c
sections.original: Age tu interea huic somnium
sections.translation: Come now , do you in the meantime relate your dream to him . I give you a substitute better than I am myself ; for what I do know , all of it I know from him .

sections.label: Section 2.2.257b
sections.original: Operam ut det .
sections.translation: Let him give his attention then .

sections.label: Section 2.2.257c
sections.original: Dabit .—
sections.translation: He ’ ll give it .

sections.label: Section 2.2.258
sections.original: Facit hic quod pauci , ut sit magistro óbsequens .
sections.translation: He does what few do , in being attentive to their masters even . ( To the COOK .) Do you give me your attention then .

sections.label: Section 2.2.259b
sections.original: Tam etsi non novi , dabo .
sections.translation: Although I don ’ t know you , I ’ ll give it you .

sections.label: Section 2.2.260
sections.original: Hac nocte in somnis visus sum viderier
sections.translation: Last night I seemed in my sleep to behold Aesculapius , seated at a distance far away from me ; and it seemed that he didn ’ t come near me , or set any value upon me .

sections.label: Section 2.2.263b
sections.original: Item alios deos facturos scilicet :
sections.translation: The other Gods will do the same , you must know ; in fact , among themselves they agree with perfect unanimity .

sections.label: Section 2.2.265
sections.original: nihil est mirandum , melius si nil fit tibi ,
sections.translation: It isn ’ t to be wondered at , if it fares no better with you . But it had been better for you to pass the night in Jove ’ s Temple , who has given you his assistance [*] in your oaths .

sections.label: Section 2.2.268
sections.original: Siquidem íncubare vélint qui periuraverint ,
sections.translation: If , indeed , those should wish to sleep there who have been guilty of perjury , it were not possible for room to be found them in the Capitol [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.2.270
sections.original: Hoc animum advorte : pacem ab Aesculapio
sections.translation: Give your attention to this ; ask peace of Aesculapius , lest perchance some great mishap befall you , which has been portended to you in your rest .

sections.label: Section 2.2.272b
sections.original: Bene facis .
sections.translation: You do well in advising me ; I ’ ll go and pray to him . ( Goes into the Temple .)

sections.label: Section 2.2.273b
sections.original: Quae res male vortat tibi .—
sections.translation: And ill speed you with it ⋯

sections.label: Section 2.2.274
sections.original: Pro di immortales , quem conspicio ? quis illic est ?
sections.translation: O immortal Gods , whom do I behold ? Who ’ s that yonder ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.275
sections.original: estne hic parasitus qui missust in Cariam ?
sections.translation: Isn ’ t that the Parasite , who was sent to Caria ? ( Goes to the door .) Hallo , come out , Phaedromus , come out , come out , come out this instant , I say .

sections.label: Section 2.2.277
sections.original: Quid istíc clamorem tollis ?
sections.translation: Why are you making this noise here ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.277b
sections.original: Parasitum tuom
sections.translation: I see your Parasite running ; see , there he is ( pointing ) , down at the end of the street [*] . Let ’ s listen from here what he ’ s about .

sections.label: Section 2.2.279b
sections.original: Sane censeo .
sections.translation: I think it ’ s as well .

sections.label: Section 2.3.280
sections.original: Dáte viam mihi , nóti atque ignoti , dúm ego hic officiúm meum
sections.translation: Known or unknown , make way for me , while here I execute my commission ; fly all of you , be off , and get out of the way , lest I should hurt any person in my speed with my head , or elbow , or breast , or with my knee . So suddenly now am I charged with a business of quickness and despatch . And be there no person ever so opulent to stop me in my way ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.285
sections.original: nec strategus nec tyrannus quisquam , nec agoranomus ,
sections.translation: neither general [*] , nor any tyrant [*] , nor market-officer [*] , nor demarch [*] nor comarch [*] , with their honors so great , but that down he goes , and tumbles head first from the footpath into the carriage-road . And then those Grecians with their cloaks , who walk about with covered heads , who go loaded beneath their cloaks with books , and with baskets [*] ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.290
sections.original: constant , conferunt sermones inter se se drapetae ,
sections.translation: they loiter together , and engage in gossipping among themselves , the gad-abouts [*] ; you may always see them enjoying themselves in the hot liquor-shops [*] ; when they have scraped up some trifle , with their covered pates they are drinking mulled wine , sad and maudlin they depart : if I stumble upon them here ,
